observations.recommendation:
We therefore recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Enjoin the Accounting and Treasury Office to reconcile their records at least quarterly, prior to the preparation of financial reports to avoid accumulation of variance, and ensure proper coordination between the Accounting and Treasury Offices to facilitate the timely recording of transactions as they occur;
Require the Accounting Office to observe Sections 3.2 to 2.4 of COA Circular 96-011 on the guidelines and procedures in the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements for all Depository Accounts; and
Direct the accounting personnel to revert back to ‘Cash-in-Bank’ account all stale checks amounting to ₱83,048.13 and recognize proper payable account. If the obligation is more than two years old, assess if these still represents valid claims, otherwise revert these to Unappropriated Surplus.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Accountant to revert the unidentified balance of ₱722,836.72 to the Unappropriated Surplus considering the number of years it stayed in the books without any claimants coming forward. On the stale checks, validate all documents available supporting the Accounts Payable outstanding for two years or more, send notices to identified creditors affected for possible claim, and if still not claimed within reasonable period of time, cause reversion to the Unappropriated Surplus pursuant to Section 98 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to initiate confirmation procedures with all NGAs and LGUs that maybe potential sources of funding for any outstanding obligation accruing to the LGU. Furthermore, conduct reconciliation of the accounts by agency and prepare accounting entries to adjust the books accordingly based on the results of the reconciliation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Conduct a thorough reconciliation of the amounts withheld from suppliers and employees’ salaries, amounts reported as remitted, and the corresponding payments supported by Official Receipts for Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR), Pag-IBIG Fund (HDMF), PhilHealth, and the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) remittances;
Require the service provider of the accounting system to create Subsidiary Ledger for every entry in the General Ledger; and
Reconcile the balances recorded in the general ledger with those reflected in the subsidiary ledgers/schedules for the accounts “Due to BIR,” “Due to Pag-IBIG,” “Due to PhilHealth,” and “Due to GSIS” to ensure the accuracy and reliability of reported balances in the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive   cease the practice of recording the unspent Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund by debiting ‘Transfer of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds’ account and crediting ‘Trust Liability – DRRMF’ account in the General Fund books unless an actual fund transfer is made in the Trust Fund books. Moreover, ensure timely transfer of unexpended LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund at year-end, in compliance with the standards.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the balances of Unexpended LDRRMF as shown in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002 for fair presentation and to enhance the understandability of the Financial Statements.

We therefore recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Realign the unobligated balance from lumpsum appropriation totaling ₱1,522,975.69 to specific program and projects in accordance with DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 1 dated November 4, 2020;
Revisit the unimplemented developmental projects and review its viability and necessity. If found to be not feasible, consider reprogramming of the fund to other developmental projects; and
Include in the Statement of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation all unimplemented projects under 20% Development fund for ease of monitoring.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require its Local Development Council to update yearly its Local Development Investment Plan (LDIP) in consultation with the Local Finance Committee and include in the LDIP projects which were found to be of priority. Moreover, ensure that all projects implemented are among the listed projects in the LDIP.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to refund the total costs of ₱69,749.16 of the project signboards not installed, and for the ensuing projects, instruct the Municipal Engineer to ensure that contractors install projects signboards, following the specifications of COA Circular No. 2013-004, at the project sites as soon as award has been made.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Transfer fund equivalent to ₱132,407.87 to the Special Trust Fund out of the General Fund to compensate the funds used from unrelated DRRM purposes; and
Strictly observe Section 5 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended that Traveling expense require the Supply Officer to submit all Inspection and Acceptance Report (IAR) to COA within 24 hours from acceptance of the delivered supplies and materials and ensure that all deliveries are coursed through the Supply/Property Officer.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) Chairperson submit the Gender and Development (GAD) Plan and Budget within five working days from its approval and the GAD Accomplishment Report within five working days from the end of January of the preceding year.

In light of the foregoing, we recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Establish and strengthen the gender-responsive monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system by issuing the necessary Executive or Administrative Order and formally constituting the GAD M&E Team;
Through the monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system, conduct regular assessment of institutional mechanisms for gender mainstreaming to monitor the effectiveness and efficiency of GAD implementation; and
Prepare and ensure the timely submission of the annual LGU Status Report on Institutional Mechanisms for Gender Mainstreaming to the DILG Regional Office.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the above-mentioned SB officials to refund immediately the overpayment of RATA totaling ₱126,560.00. Henceforth, give only a proportionate amount of RATA based on the actual services rendered.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile their records to avoid further accumulation of errors/reconciling items. Thereafter, observe the necessary monthly reconciliation for early detection of errors and/or omissions of transactions; and
Require the Accounting Office to properly observe the numbering or referencing of all JEVs and indicate RCD control numbers in the particulars column of the Cash Receipts Journals for easy reference during reconciliation.  All JEVs submitted to the Audit Team shall be approved by the Accountant.

We have recommended that management require the concerned officers to prepare and submit the BRS for all bank accounts of the Municipality.  If possible, create a task force committee from the Accounting and Treasury Offices to reconcile the balances of the Municipality’s books against the bank statements for CY 2017 going back to the last available Bank Reconciliation Statement.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the appropriate accounts by debiting the “should be” accounts and crediting the corresponding “as recorded” accounts. It is further recommended that future transactions be analyzed and classified as to the prescribed accounts in COA Circular No. 2015-009 in order to avoid misstatements and to fairly present the balances in the financial statements.

We have recommended that management require the conduct of physical inventory of PPE as required by existing regulations and submit the physical inventory reports to support the book balances of reported PPE; and
Direct the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer, as the case maybe, to maintain Property Cards per category of PPE and the Accounting Office to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for each type of PPE based on available records/supporting documents.

We have recommended that management direct the designated accountant to draw the necessary adjustments to transfer amount recorded in Due to LGU account to Trust Liabilities – DRRM;
Dr. 	Due to LGU 		Php 3,379,720.85
Cr.	Trust Liabilities –DRRM		3,379,720.85
Enforce the responsible persons to closely monitor utilization of LDRRMF so that the exact amount of unutilized balance of the 30% QRF and DRRM – MOOE will be transferred to Trust Fund books; and
Direct the accountant to return to the General Fund the excess transfer amounting to P 1,019,758.87.

We have recommended that management require the Accounting Unit to examine the completeness of the supporting documents for every transaction of the Municipality before approving the same.  If there are claims submitted to the Accounting Office for processing, but lacks the required supporting documents, it shall be returned to the claimant for proper documentation.
We have recommended that management designate a Disbursing Officer and a Liquidating Officer to ensure segregation of duties and uphold the system of checks and balances.

We have recommended that management communicate with the Municipality’s provider of accounting system if it is possible to customize the system to create special accounts under the General Fund.  In the meantime  maintain manually a complete SL for each special account in the General Fund and prepare the corresponding financial statements to form part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balance to serve as a tool in informing top management on whether or not the income generated by the economic enterprises are sufficient to meet their operating costs and enable management to evaluate them for the purpose of recommending corrective measures for the improvement of the economic enterprises.

We have recommended that management direct the BAC to immediately submit all government contracts and their supporting documents as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 within five (5) calendar days from the date of the award.

We have recommended that management require the HRMO Officer to post and fill out the vacant position of the Municipal Accountant, as prescribed in Sections 443 and 474 of RA 7160 and in accordance with existing guidelines, rules and regulations of the Civil Service Commission, for more effective administration of the Accounting Office which in turn will benefit the Municipality as a whole.

We have recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) at the end of each month pursuant to the provisions of the NGAS Manual.

observations.recommendation:

The cash advance shall be equal to the net amount of the payroll for a pay period.

Advise the municipal Accountant to stop granting of additional Cash advance to the Municipal Treasurer if there were unsettled cash balances.

Strictly follow the provision of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and COA-Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992.

Requiring the personnel concerned to should fully liquidate his cash advance and settle immediately.

Requiring the accounting in charge to facilitate the posting of liquidations made by the Officers and Employees and submit updated records for proper monitoring.

Enforcing the liquidation of cash advances thru withholding of payment of any money due to the employee, as well as to implement faithfully the policy on “No additional cash advances should be granted if existing cash advance has not been liquidated” in order that the amount will not accumulate making it difficult for the concerned officers and employees to comply the requirements of the law.

For those deceased officers and employees the Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal Accountant should exert efforts to collect from the estate and if found that it is not possible, to prepare a letter request to write off stating the reasons complete with supporting documents for verification.

And require following the provision of CSC Resolution No. 04-0676 dated June 17, 2004 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
